channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Syrian insurgents are inside Aleppo in a major setback for Assad as government forces regroup
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Thousands of Syrian insurgents fanned out inside Aleppo in vehicles with improvised armor and pickups, deploying to landmarks such as the old citadel on Saturday, a day after they entered Syria's largest city facing little resistance from government troops, according to residents and f
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Magic can't save 'Harry Potter' star Rupert Grint from a $2.3 million tax bill
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Former “Harry Potter” film actor Rupert Grint faces a 1.8 million-pound ($2.3 million) bill after he lost a legal battle with the tax authorities. Grint, who played Ron Weasley in the magical film franchise, was ordered to pay the money in 2019 after H.M. Revenue and Customs, the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about the plastic pollution crisis as treaty talks conclude in South Korea
item.description: The world’s nations will wrap up negotiating a treaty this weekend to address the global crisis of plastic pollution.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Woman accused of knife attack on family at Thanksgiving
item.description: UPDATE: The suspect, 55-year-old Lachunda Johnson has been charged with Aggravated Assault/DV, Aggravated Assault, and Domestic Assault, according to Memphis Police. MPD says Johnson is the mother of one of the stabbing victims. *** MEMPHIS, Tenn.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: 2 San Jose men arrested after joyriding in stolen vehicle: police
item.description: (KRON) -- Two San Jose residents were arrested after possessing a stolen vehicle, according to the Benicia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, NewsNation

item.title: First popularly elected Black mayor in New England, Thirman Milner, has died at 91
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former Hartford Mayor Thirman Milner, the first popularly elected Black mayor in New England, has died, the Connecticut NAACP said on Friday. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexico's congress votes to charge cruise ship passengers $42 per head for port calls
item.description: Cruise ship industry players are up in arms after the lower house of Mexico’s Congress voted this week to charge a $42 immigration levy for every passenger on cruise ships that dock in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chicago Bears fire head coach Matt Eberflus after disastrous Thanksgiving loss
item.description: The Chicago Bears have fired head coach Matt Eberflus following a six-game losing streak, with offensive coordinator Thomas Brown serving as interim coach.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Head of Ohio's teacher pension fund retires Sunday, saying fund is strong despite staff vacancies
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The outgoing head of Ohio's troubled teachers retirement system said that she is leaving the fund in strong fiscal condition, despite the turmoil at the top that her successor will inherit.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (WIAT) — Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game. Burnett Jr., a linebacker who was injured during the Magic City Classic on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, KRON4

item.title: Hot showers can harm skin, hair health, dermatologists say
item.description: Scalding showers can lead to the removal of skin layers and essential oils from your body, which can be damaging.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade: Big balloons, wet weather and 21 protesters arrested
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade marched, soared and roared into its second century on Thursday despite a drenching rain and a brief disruption from pro-Palestinian demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arizona executions to resume, breaking 2-year pause during review of state death penalty procedures
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Executions will resume in Arizona following a two-year pause, the state's top prosecutor says.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: 100 years of the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
item.description: The parade in New York City, presented by department store chain Macy's, was first held in 1924 under the heading "Macy's Christmas Parade" to promote holiday sales and spotlight the newly expanded and, at the time, largest in the world Herald Square store in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Caldwell, Stacker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade in NYC: See the balloons inflate before the big day
item.description: The 98th annual Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade will take over Manhattan Thursday morning, lining the streets with extravagant balloons and fanfare.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PHOTOS: More than $18M in fake Gibson guitars seized by U.S. Customs and Border Protection
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection seized more than 3,000 fake Gibson guitars worth nearly $19 million, had they been genuine.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Disney agrees to $43 million settlement in gender pay lawsuit
item.description: The agreement settles allegations initially made in a 2019 lawsuit filed by Disney employee LaRonda Rasmussen, who claimed she was getting paid less than her male counterparts in the same role.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: A woman was called into work on her day off. She left a lottery winner
item.description: Nobody likes being called into work on their day off. But for one California woman, a last-minute call-in would ultimately change her life for the better.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: This pyramid in Utah is a winery, a temple, and home to the mummy of a religious leader
item.description: Did you know? There's a pyramid in Salt Lake City that is a winery, a temple, and the home of the Summum religion.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: 3 Americans held for years in China have been released, White House says
item.description: Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China have been released and are returning to the United States, the White House said Wednesday, announcing a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What dynamic pricing means for Black Friday shopping
item.description: If it feels like prices are on a roller coaster ride during the holidays, you’re not imagining things.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barroso, KRON4

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump vows tariffs over immigration. What the numbers say about border crossings, drugs and crime
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a Monday evening announcement, President-elect Donald Trump railed against Mexico and Canada, accusing them of allowing thousands of people to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tank explodes, catches fire near GM facility in Michigan
item.description: A tank containing byproducts from oil drilling operations exploded and caught fire Tuesday near the General Motors Proving Ground in southeastern Michigan, damaging two nearby homes but causing no injuries, a fire chief said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Police say FBI agent sexually assaulted 2 women after promise of free tattoos, modeling
item.description: GAITHERSBURG, Md. (AP) — An FBI agent charged in Maryland with sexually assaulting two women contacted his alleged victims through social media with a promise to give them free tattoos and modeling work, police said Tuesday as they encouraged other potential victims to come forward.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tim Walz accepts turkey presentation as he eases back into his duties as Minnesota's governor
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — As feathers flew, Tim Walz accepted the symbolic presentation of a turkey Tuesday as he eases back into his duties as Minnesota's governor following the Harris-Walz ticket's defeat in the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare and Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs for millions of obese Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of Americans with obesity would be eligible to have popular weight-loss drugs like Wegovy or Zepbound covered by Medicare or Medicaid under a new rule the Biden administration proposed Tuesday morning. The costly proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia pilot, dog killed in New York plane crash; 2 other dogs found alive
item.description: A Virginia pilot and a rescue dog he was transporting were killed after a plane he was flying crashed in New York on Sunday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, Fox 5

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare, Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs like like Wegovy or Ozempic
item.description: A bipartisan coalition of congressional members have lobbied for the drugs to be covered by Medicare, saying it could save the government from spending billions of dollars on treating chronic ailments that stem from obesity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
